repo_signals: L3 smoke for hardware.pcb: layer stackup, trace impedance and length matching, return path, decoupling caps, EMI and ground plane, DRC and ERC, differential pairs
build a Kubernetes deployment with readiness probes

retrieval_query: pcb layer stackup trace impedance length matching return path decoupling capacitor emi ground plane differential pair drc erc via stitching

validation_smoke.name: hardware.pcb L3 validation smoke
signals: the controlled-impedance traces measure within tolerance of the target impedance in the fab stackup report
every high-speed net has a continuous reference plane directly beneath it across its full length
DRC and ERC complete with zero unresolved violations before release to fabrication
matched differential pairs and length-tuned buses meet the skew budget for the interface

diagnosis_smoke.name: hardware.pcb diagnosis smoke
signals: radiated emissions failing at a clock harmonic point to a broken return path or a slot in the ground plane
ringing on a fast edge indicates an impedance discontinuity from a stub, connector, or layer transition
crosstalk between adjacent traces suggests insufficient spacing or a shared return path
intermittent operation that worsens under load hints at inadequate decoupling and supply droop
a hot spot on a thermal scan reveals insufficient copper pour or missing thermal vias under a power device
